name: GORM
what_it_does: The most popular ORM library for Go. Provides struct-to-SQL mapping, automatic migrations, associations (has-one, has-many, many-to-many), transactions, hooks, and a builder API for complex queries. Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, and SQL Server. Used in Go web backends, microservices, and agent data persistence layers.
use_cases: Map Go structs to database tables for agent state persistence without writing raw SQL migrations
Query agent data (task history, tool call logs, session state) using GORM's chainable builder API
Implement agent data models with automatic timestamps, soft deletes, and relationship preloading
Run database migrations in Go agent services with GORM's AutoMigrate for schema evolution
Execute complex agent queries with GORM's raw SQL support for cases where the ORM query builder is limiting
not_for: High-performance bulk data operations — GORM's ORM overhead is significant for batch inserts/updates; use pgx or sqlx directly
Complex analytical queries — GORM's query builder can't express all SQL; use raw queries or DuckDB for analytics
Applications where SQL control and predictability are critical — ORM abstractions can generate inefficient queries; review GORM's generated SQL
best_when: You're building a Go CRUD application or agent backend with standard relational data operations and want to avoid writing repetitive SQL boilerplate.
avoid_when: You need maximum database performance or complex SQL — use sqlx or pgx directly for raw query control; GORM's overhead and generated SQL may surprise you.

auth.notes: Database ORM — connection auth is configured in DSN string. No application-level auth.

pricing.notes: MIT license. Most popular Go ORM by GitHub stars.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — local library. Performance limited by database and query complexity.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GORM errors include database error codes wrapped with context. ErrRecordNotFound is a sentinel error for missing records. Validation errors from hooks return standard Go errors.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: FirstOrCreate() is idempotent for upsert operations. Save() creates or updates by primary key. Agent code should handle ErrRecordNotFound for safe idempotent reads.
known_agent_gotchas: GORM silently ignores some errors unless you check db.Error after every operation — always chain .Error or use GORM's Error method; missing error checks lead to silent data corruption
AutoMigrate only adds columns and indexes — it never removes or modifies existing columns; schema drift from development to production requires manual migration management
Zero values (0, false, "") are treated as 'not set' in GORM updates — use map[string]interface{} or Select() to explicitly update zero-value fields
Association creation via Create() with nested structs creates all associations in a transaction — be aware of the N+1 query problem with Preload for large association sets
GORM v1 and v2 have incompatible APIs — tutorials and StackOverflow answers may use v1 syntax; always verify which version is being referenced
Raw SQL with ? placeholders is safe (parameterized), but string interpolation in Where() clauses is an SQL injection vulnerability — always use parameterized queries

security_score_components.security_notes: Use parameterized queries (GORM's default) to prevent SQL injection. DB credentials in DSN — use environment variables. Go memory safety prevents buffer overflows.
